title: Data corruption in actor/service invocation under high rps
body:
## Expected Behavior

Correct serialization (99.9% of the time) on the HTTP endpoints for actors

## Actual Behavior

When running a Proxy call for the Actor implementation, it randomly fails and spits out a weird JSON string, as if something happened on the protocol transport. Below you can find 2 different occurrences:

**Occurrence dapr/python-sdk#1:**

```bash
# /home/xanrin/dapr-python-sdk/dapr/actor/client/proxy.py (line 71)
b'[[0.056063172301151286,0.43107083116247324,-0.21845425248263262,-1.2186224010316111],1.0,true,{}]lse,{}]'

# /home/xanrin/dapr-python-sdk/dapr/serializers/json.py (line 49)
b'[[0.056063172301151286,0.43107083116247324,-0.21845425248263262,-1.2186224010316111],1.0,true,{}]lse,{}]'

# What I expect:
b'[[0.056063172301151286,0.43107083116247324,-0.21845425248263262,-1.2186224010316111],1.0,true,{}]'
```

**Occurrence dapr/python-sdk#2:**

```bash
# /home/xanrin/dapr-python-sdk/dapr/actor/client/proxy.py (line 71)
b'[[0.02180521361883352,0.9327880131478761,-0.04958054991416051,-1.4610110113961423],1.0,false,{}]]'

# /home/xanrin/dapr-python-sdk/dapr/serializers/json.py (line 49)
b'[[0.02180521361883352,0.9327880131478761,-0.04958054991416051,-1.4610110113961423],1.0,false,{}]]'

# What I expect (there is a ] too much):
b'[[0.02180521361883352,0.9327880131478761,-0.04958054991416051,-1.4610110113961423],1.0,false,{}]'
```

## Stacktrace

```bash
== APP == Process ForkServerProcess-4:
== APP == Traceback (most recent call last):
== APP ==   File "/usr/lib/python3.7/multiprocessing/process.py", line 297, in _bootstrap
== APP ==     self.run()
== APP ==   File "/usr/lib/python3.7/multiprocessing/process.py", line 99, in run
== APP ==     self._target(*self._args, **self._kwargs)
== APP ==   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.7/dist-packages/stable_baselines/common/vec_env/subproc_vec_env.py", line 18, in _worker
== APP ==     observation, reward, done, info = env.step(data)
== APP ==   File "/mnt/e/Projects/roadwork-rl/src/Lib/python/roadwork/roadwork/client/client_dapr.py", line 91, in step
== APP ==     obs, reward, done, info = asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(self.proxy.SimStep({ 'action': action }))
== APP ==   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.7/dist-packages/nest_asyncio.py", line 59, in run_until_complete
== APP ==     return f.result()
== APP ==   File "/usr/lib/python3.7/asyncio/futures.py", line 181, in result
== APP ==     raise self._exception
== APP ==   File "/usr/lib/python3.7/asyncio/tasks.py", line 249, in __step
== APP ==     result = coro.send(None)
== APP ==   File "/home/xanrin/dapr-python-sdk/dapr/actor/client/proxy.py", line 71, in __call__
== APP ==     return self._message_serializer.deserialize(rtnval, self._attr_call_type['return_types'])
== APP == Actor sending: b'[[0.056063172301151286,0.43107083116247324,-0.21845425248263262,-1.2186224010316111],1.0,true,{}]lse,{}]'
== APP == Decoding: b'[[0.056063172301151286,0.43107083116247324,-0.21845425248263262,-1.2186224010316111],1.0,true,{}]lse,{}]'
== APP ==   File "/home/xanrin/dapr-python-sdk/dapr/serializers/json.py", line 49, in deserialize
== APP == Actor sending: b'[[0.056063172301151286,0.43107083116247324,-0.21845425248263262,-1.2186224010316111],1.0,true,{}]lse,{}]'
== APP == Decoding: b'[[0.056063172301151286,0.43107083116247324,-0.21845425248263262,-1.2186224010316111],1.0,true,{}]lse,{}]'
== APP ==     obj = json.loads(data, cls=DaprJSONDecoder)
== APP ==   File "/usr/lib/python3.7/json/__init__.py", line 361, in loads
== APP ==     return cls(**kw).decode(s)
== APP ==   File "/usr/lib/python3.7/json/decoder.py", line 340, in decode
== APP ==     raise JSONDecodeError("Extra data", s, end)
== APP == json.decoder.JSONDecodeError: Extra data: line 1 column 96 (char 95)
```

## Steps to Reproduce the Problem

This happens when running an application that is utilizing a ThreadPool and running > 1000 reqs / sec (estimated)

comments.body: Adding @youngbupark since I think it's you that wrote the Actor implementation and could maybe help here? :)
Update: I am thinking it's due to aiohttp (https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/issues/3881)

> Update: I am thinking it's due to aiohttp ([aio-libs/aiohttp#3881](https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/issues/3881))

Yeah looks like it.

What do you suggest here? Seeing that it's in the Dapr code :-)

Any update on this? :)
@amanbha @msfussell @orizohar I will prioritize this bug as P0 because it is blocking one of python-users.

Thanks a lot for diving into this @youngbupark , what you say might make sense actually... sadly enough I am struggling with the issue that the calls have to be synchronous due to the Libraries I am utilizing.

The entire code base I am working on can be found here: https://github.com/Roadwork/Roadwork-RL/tree/master/src (including diagrams). When you speak about "overlapping" data instead of truncating (I've never thought of it in this way, thanks a lot for this hint!) the first thing coming to my mind is the "Nest Asyncio" library (https://pypi.org/project/nest-asyncio/) that I am utilizing to access the event loop.

I wonder if I am able to create the event loop per thread 🤔 that might indeed solve the overlapping issue then. 

What do you think? Is this the way I should be thinking about it? (again: sync is a requirement due to the third party libraries). Definitely open towards going through the problem together, but if it's overlapping I definitely understand that it's not a Dapr issue (nor asyncio one).

@XavierGeerinck let's not use nest-asyncio. Instead, try to use [asyncio.run()](https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio-task.html#asyncio.run) or [django's async_to_sync](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/59503825/django-async-to-sync-vs-asyncio-run) to make async call to sync. 

Also try to scale out the replicaset of actor services. Please update the status then I can follow it up tomorrow morning.

Thanks for the quick comment! I switched it towards asgiref's `async_to_sync` and created async methods where they should be (reference: https://github.com/Roadwork/Roadwork-RL/commit/b62e5723d6618b0bd92f80f8a4d16a82d8dfe38e) but the problem still exists.

Latest stacktrace (I marked the strange line):

@XavierGeerinck please do not create new event loop in (reference: Roadwork/Roadwork-RL@b62e572). Try to test it again.

Also please let me know how I can reproduce on my environment. Did you test it on local machine or on K8s ?

I forgot to push the latest changes :) I had indeed removed that new event loop creation already.

I tested on a local machine environment, but you should be able to reproduce it with the container installation part (https://github.com/Roadwork/Roadwork-RL/tree/master/src under "Kubernetes")
@XavierGeerinck I can reproduce it now. I am investigating this.

I captured tcp traffic of server and client side with wireshark
> You can open the captured packets on wireshark by downloading this [high_freq.pcapng.gz](https://github.com/dapr/python-sdk/files/4963842/high_freq.pcapng.gz)

Based on the investigation, actorservice returned correct json format response when proxy called actor and its method. but when dapr sidecar for actor client returned the corrupted response data to proxy client.

For example, two invocation requests, such as A and B, come to dapr sidecar for invocation in a row with almost several ns time gap, where A comes first and B next. actorservice returns the response for B and then response for A. but B's response body is same as A's response body, but no header change. B's headers are not changed and content-length is correct for B's original body. 

so when proxy tried to deserialize B's response body, it was failed because B's response body was A's and B's data was truncated or B's data included garbage data. 

We will investigate this issue more and fix runtime as soon as possible.
cc/ @yaron2 @amanbha @LMWF @yolember
@XavierGeerinck I found the root cause of this issue and will fix the bug and release 0.9.1 tomorrow.
Awesome! Amazing work, you rock! Looking forward to see what was causing this 😊
@XavierGeerinck I transferred this to dapr/dapr because this is the runtime bug
Good we found it, but this does not need a 0.9.1 release and is not under the definition of P0.

The perf test doesn't include actors. That's part of 0.10 work.

The bug is in app channel so the same issue happens in service invocation under high load scenario (1000+ rps)

No, they can only verify status codes of the response.

> 
> No, they can only verify status codes of the response.

Should, they should test correctness too? We can add a variation to the tests which can be run as a scale test.

> > > > > > > Good we found it, but this does not need a 0.9.1 release and is not under the definition of P0.
> > > > > > 
> > > > > > 
> > > > > > Ok make sense. @XavierGeerinck i will provide the way to use the fix.
> > > > > > @yaron2 btw, are we validating response for each call in invocation perf test ? Then this would have to be discoverable..
> > > > > 
> > > > > 
> > > > > The perf test doesn't include actors. That's part of 0.10 work.
> > > > 
> > > > 
> > > > The bug is in app channel so the same issue happens in service invocation under high load scenario (1000+ rps)
> > > 
> > > 
> > > Can perf tests perform deserialization of response and ensure it succeeds.
> > 
> > 
> > No, they can only verify status codes of the response.
> 
> Should, they should test correctness too? We can add a variation to the tests which can be run as a scale test.

I think correctness should not be under the umbrella of performance tests. For example, based on the research I made in performance tests it's highly advisable that the app you are testing never returns a payload back unless you're doing something explicit with the data, instead having the direction go one way to the app.

> > > > > > > > Good we found it, but this does not need a 0.9.1 release and is not under the definition of P0.
> > > > > > > 
> > > > > > >

> 
> I think correctness should not be under the umbrella of performance tests. For example, based on the research I made in performance tests it's highly advisable that the app you are testing never returns a payload back unless you're doing something explicit with the data, instead having the direction go one way to the app.

I understand, how do you propose to uncover these scenarios? perhaps run a variation which does just scale testing without doing perf measurement?
Yes, it probably means a different/new kind of test.
Interesting. If we check only status code in perf test, how can we convince users to trust our result? Perf test is valid only when functionality is correctly working with the certain condition.

> Interesting. If we check only status code in perf test, how can we convince users to trust our result? Perf test is valid only when functionality is correctly working with the certain condition.

That's not correct, because you are not taking into consideration the goal of our perf test.

The purpose of the current perf test (service invocation) is to report the added latency of the call to the Dapr sidecar. That has nothing to do with the data returned. That is also why the test app does not return a data payload, because it is not part of the test. It just returns a status code to signal that the app has received the call and responded back.

If we create a perf test that has an explicit goal of reporting that the data returned from an app is serialized correctly under a given load, then that would be correct, but in that case as I mentioned above, it's a new test that has a new goal to report.

As I understand, the scenario can be covered by just running other variation of the same perf test which also does validation of the response(could be based on some flag which controls this behavior). If the response is incorrect fail the test.
Yep.

@XavierGeerinck we merged the fix. You can use this edge version of dapr.

Selfhost mode:
1. dapr uninstall --all
2. dapr init
3. download your edge version of daprd binary from https://github.com/dapr/dapr/actions/runs/180628900
4. replace daprd with edge version 

Kubernetes with helm:
```
helm install helm dapr/dapr --namespace=dapr-system --set-string global.tag=edge
```

Awesome, thanks! It worked!! :) I ran 8 processes @ ~42 req / s resulting in 100k steps in 294s without a crash!

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/591282/88374796-b1ced000-cd9a-11ea-9a7a-5b23e75632ca.png)
